response: The internet began in the 1960s with US Department of Defense networking research, leading to ARPANET’s first message in 1969 and the adoption of TCP/IP in 1983, which established the modern internet’s foundation. Tim Berners-Lee’s creation of the World Wide Web in 1989 and the release of the Mosaic browser in 1993 opened the internet to mainstream users. After the 1990s dot-com boom and 2001 crash, companies like Amazon and Google became dominant, while the rise of social media and smartphones in the mid-2000s made the internet central to daily communication. Today, more than 5 billion people use the internet, which supports global commerce, communication, entertainment, and cloud computing.
